channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Russia hits Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles, killing 1 and injuring 31
item.description: Russia has launched a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, killing one person and injuring at least 31. Local authorities report damage across six districts.
item.content:encoded:
Russia has launched a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, killing one person and injuring at least 31. Local authorities report damage across six districts.

(Image credit: Evgeniy Maloletka)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Denise Powell wins Democratic primary for Nebraska's 2nd congressional district
item.description: Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.
item.content:encoded:
Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.

(Image credit: Rebecca S. Gratz)
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow

item.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
item.description: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.
item.content:encoded:
Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.

(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Foreign ticket holders from World Cup teams' countries won't have to pay bonds to enter U.S.
item.description: The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next chair of the Federal Reserve
item.description: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.
item.content:encoded:
Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Neanderthals may have drilled out a cavity 59,000 years ago
item.description: Scientists dug up a Paleolithic tooth that shows signs that these hominins may have been capable of executing a precise dental procedure.
item.content:encoded:
Scientists dug up a Paleolithic tooth that shows signs that these hominins may have been capable of executing a precise dental procedure.

(Image credit: Zubova et al.)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Greetings from Seville, where springtime means caracoles
item.description: Spring is snail season in Seville. Caracoles in southern Spain differ from the well-known French escargot — they're smaller and eaten directly from the shell. And everyone has a favorite tapas bar that serves them.
item.content:encoded: Spring is snail season in Seville. Caracoles in southern Spain differ from the well-known French escargot — they're smaller and eaten directly from the shell. And everyone has a favorite tapas bar that serves them.
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: Alex Murdaugh will get a new murder trial. Here's a timeline of his case
item.description: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded:
Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.

(Image credit: Gavin McIntyre)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate as police try to arrest senator
item.description: Witnesses say a burst of gunfire has rung out in the Philippine Senate where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the ICC.
item.content:encoded:
Witnesses say a burst of gunfire has rung out in the Philippine Senate where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the ICC.

(Image credit: Aaron Favila)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: War in Iran costs $29 billion so far. And, students are finally improving in math
item.description: Pentagon officials told Congress they estimate the war in Iran has cost $29 billion so far. And, a new Education Scorecard shows U.S. students are improving in math.
item.content:encoded:
Pentagon officials told Congress they estimate the war in Iran has cost $29 billion so far. And, a new Education Scorecard shows U.S. students are improving in math.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton
